title: Podman exec API leaks conmon processes
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When using the podman API to launch processes with exec a conmon process is left behind on the host and remains even after the process in the container completes. The processes appear to be eventually cleaned up. 

This leak does not happen when using the podman CLI.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Launch a long running container

> $ podman run -d ubi8/ubi:latest /usr/bin/sleep 999

2. Obtain the container ID

> $ podman ps --format "{{.ID}}"
> 3d806b972a91

3. Launch an exec

> $ CONTAINER_ID=3d806b972a91
> $ PODMAN_SOCKET=/run/user/$(id -u)/podman/podman.sock
> $ EXEC_ID=$(curl -X POST -s --unix-socket "$PODMAN_SOCKET" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' "http://d/v1.0.0/libpod/containers/$CONTAINER_ID/exec" -d '{"Cmd": ["/usr/bin/sleep", "2"], "AttachStdout": true}' | jq -r '.Id' )
> $ curl -X POST --unix-socket "$PODMAN_SOCKET" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' "http://d/v1.0.0/libpod/exec/$EXEC_ID/start" -d '{"Detatch": false}'

4. Observe that two conmon processes are leaked per each exec. Repeat step 3 for more leaks. Note that they eventually get cleaned up (presumably by the --exit-delay 300)

```
$ ps -aux
myuser  534770 96.0  0.0 143860  2328 ?        Ssl  15:24   4:22 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d -u
myuser  534786  0.0  0.0 143860   828 ?        S    15:24   0:00 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d -u
myuser  534894  0.0  0.0 143860  2292 ?        Ssl  15:25   0:00 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d -u
myuser  534915  0.0  0.0 143860   828 ?        S    15:25   0:00 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d -u
```

The full process:

> myuser  534938  0.0  0.0 143860  2332 ?        Ssl  15:25   0:00 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d -u 1b3f16b18aa89ca0d1907568bfbd8f28e2f07684f045e318532870148d1b5980 -r /usr/bin/runc -b /var/appl/jenkins/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d/userdata/1b3f16b18aa89ca0d1907568bfbd8f28e2f07684f045e318532870148d1b5980 -p /var/appl/jenkins/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d/userdata/1b3f16b18aa89ca0d1907568bfbd8f28e2f07684f045e318532870148d1b5980/exec_pid -n busy_varahamihira --exit-dir /var/appl/jenkins/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d/userdata/1b3f16b18aa89ca0d1907568bfbd8f28e2f07684f045e318532870148d1b5980/exit --full-attach -l none --log-level info --runtime-arg --log-format=json --runtime-arg --log --runtime-arg=/var/appl/jenkins/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d/userdata/1b3f16b18aa89ca0d1907568bfbd8f28e2f07684f045e318532870148d1b5980/oci-log -e --exec-attach --exec-process-spec /var/appl/jenkins/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d/userdata/1b3f16b18aa89ca0d1907568bfbd8f28e2f07684f045e318532870148d1b5980/exec-process-4160272710 --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /var/appl/jenkins/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/78572/containers --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg info --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg cgroupfs --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/78572/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --network-config-dir --exit-command-arg  --exit-command-arg --network-backend --exit-command-arg cni --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg runc --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg file --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg --rm --exit-command-arg --exec --exit-command-arg 1b3f16b18aa89ca0d1907568bfbd8f28e2f07684f045e318532870148d1b5980 --exit-command-arg 3d806b972a91eca7ad224b3998f072c0c0e20ea34ce430ba9dfcbcc5dcbd9f5d --exit-delay 300

**Describe the results you received:**
Two conmon processes are left running after the exec finishes

**Describe the results you expected:**
The processes terminate after the exec completes. This is how the podman CLI acts.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.17.7

Built:      Tue Apr 19 06:16:32 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 78572
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 200000
      size: 100000
  kernel: 4.18.0-372.9.1.el8.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: k8s-file
  memFree: 376504320
  memTotal: 3913019392
  networkBackend: cni
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.3-2.module+el8.6.0+14877+f643d2d6.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.3
      spec: 1.0.2-dev
      go: go1.17.7
      libseccomp: 2.5.2
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/user/78572/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
        seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: true
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.8-2.module+el8.6.0+14877+f643d2d6.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.8
      commit: d361001f495417b880f20329121e3aa431a8f90f
      libslirp: 4.4.0
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.2
  swapFree: 2071588864
  swapTotal: 2147479552

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-4.0.2-6.module+el8.6.0+14877+f643d2d6.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

This is the latest version available for RHEL8

comments.body:
I commented on this one via email, but apparently Github lost is. Pasting:

This is deliberate, if a bit of an ugly hack. Basically, Podman needs to retain the exec session in the database for 5 minutes after it exits for Docker compat. We don’t have a daemon, so the easiest way to do this is to have Conmon wait 5 minutes before cleaning up the database and exiting.

I hope to remove the need for this in the future when we move to Conmon-rs.

Going to leave this open given that we do hope to fix it eventually, but retag as a feature.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This is deliberate, if a bit of an ugly hack. Basically, Podman needs to
retain the exec session in the database for 5 minutes after it exits for
Docker compat. We don’t have a daemon, so the easiest way to do this is to
have Conmon wait 5 minutes before cleaning up the database and exiting.

I hope to remove the need for this in the future when we move to Conmon-rs.
